titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick Carey to the District Manager, Post Office Telephones, Cork, 15 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick Carey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Patrick Carey to the District Manager, Post Office Telephones, Cork regarding payment orders. Carey reminds the district manage that the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians are no longer responsible for the calls of the Presbytery and Dr O'Sullivan.
Patrick Carey (1853-1934) was the Union Clerk for the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians. Poor law unions and their workhouses were established by the Poor Law (Ireland) Act, 1838, as a response to widespread poverty, and were each governed by a Board of Guardians. This letter comes from the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians Minute Book.

msIdentifier.collection: Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians BG104/B/4

correspAction.placeName: Post Office, South Mall, Cork, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
15th Decr 1915, Excess Calls. Dear Sir, Referring to your Communication of yesterday
I enclose Paying Order for £1.19.0 as requested in your
letter of yesterday 19 11 15 . Will you kindly inform me how
the £3 cashed by you on the 29th June has been dealt
with. I need scarcely remind you that the Guardians
are not longer responsible for the calls of the
Presbytery and Dr O'Sullivan. I am, Sir,
Your Obedient Servant. Clerk of Union. The Supt for Ireland, The Dist Mgr
Post Office Telephones,
General Post Office, South Mall
Dublin, Cork
